title: Oedipus the King: Know Thyself
description: The moment of recognition: Oedipus discovers he has killed his father and married his mother.
introduction: Aristotle called Oedipus the King the perfect tragedy. The play dramatizes the most terrifying form of knowledge: the discovery that the truth you have been seeking is the truth about yourself. Oedipus, the great solver of riddles, cannot solve the riddle of his own identity until it is too late. The Delphic maxim 'Know Thyself' becomes not wisdom but catastrophe.

translation.original_text: ἰοὺ ἰού· τὰ πάντ᾿ ἂν ἐξήκοι σαφῆ. ὦ φῶς, τελευταῖόν σε προσβλέψαιμι νῦν, ὅστις πέφασμαι φύς τ᾿ ἀφ᾿ ὧν οὐ χρῆν, ξὺν οἷς τ᾿ οὐ χρῆν ὁμιλῶν, οὕς τέ μ᾿ οὐκ ἔδει κτανών.
translation.text: Ah! Ah! It all comes clear now! O light, let me look upon you for the last time — I who have been revealed as born from those I should not have been born from, living with those I should not have lived with, and having killed those I should never have killed.

translation.original_text: ὥστε θνητὸν ὄντα κείνην τὴν τελευταίαν ἰδεῖν ἡμέραν ἐπισκοποῦντα μηδέν᾿ ὀλβίζειν, πρὶν ἂν τέρμα τοῦ βίου περάσῃ μηδὲν ἀλγεινὸν παθών.
translation.text: So count no mortal happy until he has passed the final limit of his life without suffering grief.
